content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

February 21, 1985


Melvyn W. Price
Supervising Deputy County Counsel
County of Sacramento
700 H Street, Suite 2650
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
     Our Advice No. A-85-012

Dear Mr. Price:

     This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the 1984 amendments to Government Code Section
84308 1/ and the effect on the decisions of the planning
commission.

FACTS

     In a previous advice letter concerning Section 84308 (No.
A-83-080), we concluded that a planning commission makes a
decision within the meaning of Section 84308 when:

     1. Acting in its advisory capacity, the planning
commission votes on a recommendation to the Board of
Supervisors; and

     2. Acting in its decisionmaking capacity, the planning
commission votes on a matter.

     Among other changes to Section 84308, the word "final" was
inserted before the word "decision." See Section 84308(b) and
(d).

_________________________

1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise specified. The amendments are in Stats. 1984,
Ch. 1681.

Melvyn W. Price
February 21, 1985
Page 2


QUESTION

Does the term "final decision" in Section 84308 as amended
change the conclusions reached in our previous advice letter?

DISCUSSION

The insertion of the word "final" in Section 84308 does not
change the meaning or application of the word "decision" from
that given to it previously in our advice letter; it was
intended to clarify the calculation of the time limits in the
statute. Accordingly, when a planning commission votes on a
recommendation or on another matter, it has made a "final
decision" within the meaning of Section 84308 even if there will
be a subsequent decision in the same matter made by the Board of
Supervisors.

If I may be of any further assistance, or if you have any
additional questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane Maude Fishburn
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh

COUNTY OF SACRAMENTO

JAN 23 OFFICE OF THE COUNTY COUNSEL Supervising Deputy County Counsel
Thomas A. Darling
700 H Street, Suite 2650
Sacramento, CA 95814 Robert L. Perrse
Clement J Dougherty Jr
Phone: (916) 440-5544 Louis J Fine
Morey L Fisher
Melvyn W. Price

January 24, 1985
Deputy County Counsel
L.B. Elam Richard D. Mayer
County Counsel J. Steven Blank
Lawrence A Jisnes
Fred G. Williams William M. Fowler
Assistant County Counsel Elaine F DiPietro
Frank M. Garcia
Manuel E Lopes
James J. Oswald
Margaret L Hagerty
Anthony L Wright
Barry Steiner
Kathryn A Shmell
Richard G Lolla
John H Dodds
Kathleen A O'Connor
Barbara Milman Denis J Zell
Chief - Legal Division Ramona A. Armstead
Fair Political Practices Commission Paul Reynaga
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: The Time Periods During Which
§84308 Restricts the Activities
of Planning Commissioners

Dear Ms. Milman:

By your advice letter No. A-83-080, dated April 25, 1983, you
opined as follows:

1) Where a decision of a planning commission is not final,
but merely a recommendation to the Board of Supervisors, the
"decision" is made when the vote is taken as to the
recommendation.

2) Where the decision of the planning commission is final,
the "decision" is made when the commission votes on the matter.

Consequently, you concluded that the time periods that are
set forth in §84308 are measured from these "decisions". That
is, the requirements of disclosure and disqualification would
apply until the date of that decision and the prohibition against
accepting a contribution in the amount of $250 or more would
continue for a period of three months after the date of that
"decision".

However, the 1984 amendment of §84308 indicates that said
section is applicable while a matter is pending before the agency
and for three months following the date a "final decision" is
rendered in the proceeding.

Barbara Milman                    -2-                    January 24, 1985


     We now inquire whether the term "final decision" would cause
you to change the referenced opinion rendered in advice letter
No. A-83-080.

                                                Very truly yours,

                                                L. B. ELAN
                                                County Counsel

                                                [signature]
                                                MELVYN W. PRICE
                                                Supervising Deputy

MWP:sd

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660         322-5901           322-6441              322-6444

January 29, 1985


Melvyn W. Price
Supervising Deputy
700 H Street, Suite 2650
Sacramento, CA  95814

                                                Re: A-85-012

Dear Mr. Price:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to Diane Maura Fishburn, an
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at
(916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        General Counsel

BAM:nwm
